device_name: Samsung ECG Monitor Application with Irregular Heart Rhythm Notification
intended_use: The Samsung ECG Monitor Application with Irregular Heart Rhythm Notification is an over-the-counter (OTC) software-only, mobile medical application operating on a compatible Samsung Galaxy Watch and P
intended_use_full: The Samsung ECG Monitor Application with Irregular Heart Rhythm Notification is an over-the-counter (OTC) software-only, mobile medical application operating on a compatible Samsung Galaxy Watch and Phone for informational use only in adults 22 years and older. The app analyzes pulse rate data to identify episodes of irregular heart rhythms suggestive of atrial fibrillation (AFib) and provides a notification suggesting the user record an ECG to analyze the heart rhythm. Following this prompt, or based on the user's own initiative, the app is intended to create, record, store, transfer, and display a single-channel ECG, similar to a Lead I ECG. Classifiable traces are labeled by the app as either AFib or sinus rhythm with the intention of aiding heart rhythm identification.
target_condition: Atrial fibrillation (AFib) or irregular heart rhythms suggestive of AFib
ground_truth: Clinician-adjudicated and cardiologist-reviewed patch ECG data (reference ECG patch worn simultaneously for approximately seven days)
other_metrics: Subject-level sensitivity 95% CI: 60.5%-75.5%; Subject-level specificity 95% CI: 98.0%-99.6%; Tachogram-level PPV 95% CI: 94.7%-96.7%; AFib ECG classification sensitivity: 98.1%; Sinus rhythm ECG classification specificity: 100%
